evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Own the quality, availability, and trustworthiness of data — through quality checks and data contracts
evidence.domain_risk: Design solutions for KPIs and business metrics
evidence.collaboration_width: Engage with analysts and scientists to understand problems and translate them into data solutions; Work with developers on data creation aligned with business logic
evidence.data_team_maturity: Provide expertise and collaborate with stakeholders to develop new data products within a Data Mesh environment
evidence.jd_authorship: Own the quality, availability, and trustworthiness of data — through quality checks and data contracts
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build an environment where self-service capabilities are available for analysts and data consumers
evidence.autonomy_level: Provide expertise and collaborate with stakeholders to develop new data products within a Data Mesh environment
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: develop new data products within a Data Mesh environment
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Developing new data products signals greenfield creation, but doing so within an established Data Mesh environment also implies extending existing platform infrastructure, making the mode mixed.
evidence.ai_role: Design solutions for KPIs and business metrics
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI signals present; role focuses on standard data engineering and product development without AI tools, infrastructure, or AI-first practices.
evidence.testing_framing: Own the quality, availability, and trustworthiness of data — through quality checks and data contracts
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Explicit ownership responsibility verb 'own' paired with quality checks and data contracts indicates candidate owns quality practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Own the quality, availability, and trustworthiness of data
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability concerns (quality, availability, trustworthiness) emphasized without explicit regulatory or compliance dominance.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: No velocity signals present; multiple rigour signals including data contracts, quality checks, and ownership of trustworthiness.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: JD focuses on business metrics and data products without explicit regulatory, financial reporting, or compliance language.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Data Mesh architecture indicates an established, sophisticated data function, while the emphasis on actively developing new products and building self-service capabilities suggests they remain in a growth/scaling phase.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Technical specificity (data contracts, data mesh architecture, self-service capabilities) indicates hiring manager input, but generic collaboration language and boilerplate action verbs suggest significant recruiter contribution.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves the data platform itself and internal data consumers (analysts, scientists, developers), not external commercial, product, or finance functions.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Combines strategic development and ownership of data products with collaborative execution supporting analysts and scientists in a shared environment.
